title: Amazon Basics Electric Coffee Grinder, Coffee Bean and Spice Grinder, With Heavy-Duty Stainless Steel Grind Blades
description:
About this item
Electric coffee bean grinder appliance for home use
Can grind 30 grams/1 ounce of beans for drip coffee brewing in 10 seconds
Grind coffee beans, herbs, spices, grains, nuts, and more
Clear safety lid allows you to easily see the results in progress
Heavy-duty stainless steel grind blade
Compact design perfect for countertop use
reviews_5s:
Looks modern and feels substantial, like a more expensive branded product. It is basic in its functionality, as there’s no automatic timer, and the small size is only good for personal usage (not for multiple people or large pots). The motor is fairly quiet but very powerful. Cleaning is no worse than any other grinders, and the small size makes it quicker. If you enjoy prepping and making coffee the way you like it, then this is easily a great buy.I purchased this little grinder to try it out for freshly grinding flax seeds. I wanted to try Budwig and read that ground flax seeds start to become rancid 20 minutes after grinding. I wasn't sure if the taste of the oil and ground seeds would be something I would be willing to do for the long haul, so I opted for this inexpensive grinder.

This little grinder is intuitive and easy to operate. Take off the clear top, add the seeds, pop the top on, careful to align the button on the lid with the base, press the lid/button and it takes off! Within a very few seconds I have finely ground flax.

I was pleased that the noise level wasn't bad at all and was actually much less than I expected. However, coffee beans may be louder to grind. The actual sound of the machine is a light whirring sound.

Capacity is very adequate for my needs. I only grind 1 to 2 tablespoons of flax seeds at a time. The grinding does make them fluffy and take up more space, but I still have plenty of room. The idea is to only grind only as much as is immediately needed anyway, so that it is as fresh as possible.

I'm a newbie with a grinder so I'm not sure how much cleaning should be done. The only action I've taken toward semi cleaning it is to wipe it out a little with a paper towel. I don't see where the base comes apart for cleaning the blade and little bowl inside, but I think these are meant to be dedicated pretty much to grinding one thing anyway, and wiping out the residue with a paper towel was effective.

This definitely met my needs and I would recommend this to others.Never really knew how to use this product or even thought that I would need one or even purchase one until I started trying different coffees. This grinder is easy to use and grinds the coffee in seconds. If anyone is looking to buy this and is not sure if they will know how to use it, trust me, it is very easy and works great. Well worth the money!!!I purchased this grinder around a week ago to grind spices at home. I have used it once and it has worked very effectively. I used it to grind a mix of black and green cardamoms, coriander seeds, star anis, cinnamon sticks, and black pepper. I was concerned that it may not be able to grind the fibers in cardamom husk and I might have to use a strainer to remove the husks but it was able to grind everything that I put in to a fine powder. It feels pretty robust and is very quiet during operation. The grinding jar does not separate so you can not wash it with water; I wipe it with a
a wet cloth after use. Since I am going to use it only for spices I do not see that as a big problem. However, even after wiping, the smell of the spices lingers so I will not use it for grinding things where smell might be an issue (e.g. coffee beans).The grinder is powerful, working quickly to grind coffee, rice, and beans finely. Whether you're making smoothies, stews, or even personal beauty supplies, this is a welcome and reliable accessory. The sound is relatively low too. And, it comes at a great value.Works like a charm, grinding coffee beans into fine powder for brewing. We love it.Just when my bean counter was relaxing because my last grinder had died… I bought a new and better one!

If you like fresh ground coffee, even your bean counter will be happy with this purchase! I’m not sure you can find a better coffee grinder for the priceI haven’t had it for very long so can’t speak to its longevity. But this grinder has plenty of power and sharp blades to quickly grind the handful of coffee beans I toss into it every morning-about ten seconds does the trick. I appreciate it’s small footprint too-it doesn’t take up much space on a countertop. I use a soft small nylon bristle brush to clean any grounds that are trapped under the blades or in the receptacle. Lightweight unit, but seems well made.Grinds coffee beans perfectly. Three scoops of beans are easily accommodated in the grinder to make 12 cups of coffee each morning. Operating sound is not too loud or annoying.Easy, space saving, ground just what you need and no more. Two thumbs up.
reviews_1s:
The problem with this grinder is where the power button is and how it's activated. The lid of the grinder has large slide button that is supposed to turn the grinder on when pushed down, the problem is this slide works by moving a small nub down to make contact with the button on the base. What seems to happen after every use is the button gets blocked by coffee grounds which prevents the plastic nub on the grinder lid from making contact with the button. A coffee grinder is not something I would clean often but with this one you really have no choice.

It's also extremely messy probably because the lid is a little to big for the base.

Also I noticed an electrical smell when grinding for a fine grain.

Do yourself a favor spend the extra 10 bucks for a grinder with a better design.It ground okay but the button in the lid that is supposed to engage the switch to operate it was temperamental to begin with and stopped working within a few days. I would return it but can’t find the box it came in. Rather than risk another faulty one I got a Mr. Coffee grinder that was one sale fit about the same price, regularly $29, and it has been working great. I ordinarily like Amazon Basic products, but this one disappointed.
Note added: after resolving lots of other hectic life stuff and health problems that prevented me from getting out to the UPS store with many intended returns that I just ate the cost of, I decided to see if I could at least get this replaced as defective, since I’d let the refund window go by because I couldn’t find the box or packing slip for a return. Figured if I got a non-defective one, I could use it for grinding spices instead of coffee. However, when I tried to follow the links for a warranty replacement (it shipped June 29, so it’s def. too late for a return, but I did post this review originally within a week of receipt and I haven’t used it since then), the “contact customer service” link kept dumping me on a page saying that customer service is overwhelmed right now and I couldn’t get any further in the process. It’s only $17 but I’ve been a really good Amazon customer this year (bought over $3000 in household stuff this summer alone, from expensive bike locks to inflatable hot tubs, door locks, flat-pack cabinets and outdoor gazebos), as well as a bunch of electronics from winter into spring, and I was hoping they’d be a little more available to help me get my money’s worth.This is a one-star review for the Amazon Basics Electric Coffee Grinder. I purchased this product expecting it to be a versatile tool for grinding both coffee beans and spices, as advertised. Unfortunately, my experience has been quite disappointing.

First and foremost, the grinder is not suitable for grinding spices. The blades are not designed for the tougher texture of spices, and they quickly become clogged and inefficient. This renders the product useless for one of its primary advertised purposes.

Secondly, the grinder is not leak-proof. When grinding coffee beans, fine particles often escape from the lid and spill onto the counter. This is both messy and inconvenient, making it difficult to use the grinder without a cleanup afterwards.

Given these significant flaws, I cannot recommend this product. It falls short of its advertised capabilities and is not a practical tool for grinding either coffee beans or spices.I was hoping this would be so much better! I bought it as a secondary grinder for my espresso machine so we could grind decaf in the evenings. I guess I totally missed the mark. No measurement, no grind control/consistency, hard to clean, it’s an overall terrible machine and an epic waste of money. Unfortunately, I failed to test when we received and am now also out of our return window. Avoid if purchasing for coffee/espresso.Issue #1 - the lid is designed to jam at an awkward angle more often than to fit in the closed position. Once closed, there is so much slack and wobble, one has to look to make sure it is actually closed.

Issue #2 - upon opening the grinder, one is met with an overpowering odor of machine oil and plastic out-gassing. You will need to clean it thoroughly and air it out before using it. This will not be easy given the close quarters and sharp un-removable blade in the middle.

Issue #3 - the power cord was designed by someone with special needs and no clue about basic design principles. It seems intentionally designed so the cord will always be caught under the grinder, preventing it from sitting flat. And the incorporated storage 'feature' for the cord is preposterous and simply does not work. So after tediously winding the cord, it immediately unwinds when you let go if it.

Issue #4 - I live in a small town, and the nearest Staples is more than a 30 minute drive from me. Yet the ONLY way I can return this piece of garbage WITHOUT having to pay for the 'privilege', is to drop it as a Staples store. If I want to use a UPS drop, i have to pay fort it.

If you dislike someone, get this for them. Otherwise, don't get this.The way the lid fits the body of the grinder is terrible, always makes a mess. I regret buying this. You know the mess is coming, you do everything you can to prevent it, it still happens when you open it. Terrible.bad design and it broke after a while . issue happens if you drip the lid. the lid then breaks into two pieces and i was not able to re-attach the two places. therefore it was no longer usable. now i need to get a better made product. unacceptable.The title and description says this is safe for grinding spices and nuts... the instructions on the other hand say it is NOT safe for grinding food items, spices, or nuts. Most people probably ignore instructions so this could cause food safety issues.Machine never ground evenly. The cord doesn’t allow the item to sit flush to the counter and now it died. In am so happy to put this junk in the trash and buy a name brand grinder!Avoid at all cost. The top cannot be removed to be washed and is cheap with the flimsy plastic around the bowl that holds the items to ground. This makes it almost unusable if you are trying to grind multiple herbs or coffee beans throughout the day. I had salt stuck within the blades and you cannot clean it easily. It is beyond cheap. Find something/anything better.
